Eos Energy Enterprises Files 8K - Other Events >EOSE
American Airlines Group Inc Profit Retreats In Q2
VMM: Strong revenue and profit growth, robust private brand performance, and expanding Quick Commerce
data.description:
Revenue grew 18.7% year-over-year to INR 3,727 crore, with 10% same-store sales growth and 25.6% PAT increase. Private brands drove 75% of revenue, Quick Commerce expanded, and RFID rollout advanced. Inflation remains a risk, but margin and growth outlook are positive.

Based on

data.title: ECB Pause Seen as Temporary — Market Talk
LightPath Technologies to Sell China Subsidiary for $4.5 Million, Sets 5-Year Supply and IP Terms
data.description:
LightPath Technologies entered into an Equity Transfer Agreement to sell 100% of its LightPath (Zhenjiang) Optical Instrumentation subsidiary to Hengtu Optical Technology, a management-led buyer, for $4.5 million. The price is payable over up to five years with at least $500,000 annually, 4% financing interest on installments, and 7% damages on overdue amounts. LightPath receives board observer rights and post-closing restrictions, plus limited trademark and technology licenses. For five years, the buyer will supply products to LightPath at cost plus 10%, increasing by 5% for each $1 million of principal repaid, up to cost plus 30% when fully paid.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Equity transfer agreement (sale of 100% of China subsidiary)
  • Counterparty: Hengtu Optical Technology and Leo Zheng
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 23 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Up to 5 years
  • Reason: Divest Chinese subsidiary and formalize ongoing supply and licensing

Original SEC Filing: LIGHTPATH TECHNOLOGIES INC [ LPTH ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Ladder Capital Q2 2026: GAAP income $16.3M, distributable EPS $0.24
data.description:
Ladder Capital reported GAAP income before taxes of $16.3 million and diluted EPS of $0.12 for the quarter ended June 30, 2026, while distributable earnings were $30.8 million, or distributable EPS of $0.24. The company said book value remained stable as it grew its loan portfolio and generated gains across its multi-cylinder platform. Ladder Capital will host a conference call and made supplemental investor materials available on its IR website.

Financial Highlights

  • Income before taxes (GAAP): $16.3 million for Q2 2026.
  • Diluted earnings per share (GAAP): $0.12 for Q2 2026.
  • Distributable earnings: $30.8 million for Q2 2026.
  • Distributable EPS: $0.24 for Q2 2026 (after-tax distributable earnings $29.82 million).
  • Total assets: $5.606 billion as of June 30, 2026; total liabilities: $4.1796 billion; total equity: $1.4267 billion.

Business Highlights

  • Loan portfolio growth: Mortgage loans receivable increased to $2.790 billion (net of allowance) as of June 30, 2026, from $2.217 billion at December 31, 2025, reflecting origination activity and portfolio expansion.
  • Multi-cylinder platform performance: Continued generation of gains across lending, real estate operations and securities investments contributed to distributable earnings growth.
  • Real estate operations: Real estate and related lease intangibles, net increased to $776.5 million, with real estate operating income of $30.9 million for the quarter.
  • Capital and funding: Debt obligations, net were $4.0049 billion as of June 30, 2026, supporting lending and investment activities.
  • Corporate positioning: Management and the board maintain significant insider ownership and the company retains investment-grade ratings from Moody’s (Baa3) and Fitch (BBB-) with stable outlooks, and S&P (BB+) with a positive outlook.

Original SEC Filing: Ladder Capital Corp [ LADR ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Inhibikase Therapeutics granted FDA orphan drug designation for IKT-001 in PAH
data.description:
Inhibikase Therapeutics announced FDA orphan drug designation for IKT-001 for the treatment of PAH.

Key Highlights:

  • FDA’s Office of Orphan Products Development granted Orphan Drug Designation to IKT-001 for PAH.
  • Designation applies to the active moiety imatinib, offering potential tax credits and fee exemptions.
  • ODD may provide up to seven years of U.S. market exclusivity upon approval.
  • Inhibikase says preclinical data showed improved pulmonary and hemodynamic markers with lower GI toxicity potential vs imatinib.
  • IKT-001 is being evaluated in the pivotal Phase 3 IMPROVE-PAH trial, which is actively enrolling (~180 sites worldwide).

Original SEC Filing: Inhibikase Therapeutics, Inc. [ IKT ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Eos Energy reports $37.7M gross from rights offering; supports $263M raised for Frontier Power USA
data.description:
Eos Energy reported $37.7 million in expected gross proceeds from its expired rights offering, contributing to approximately $263 million raised for Frontier Power USA.

Key Highlights:

  • Rights offering expired July 21, 2026; unexercised rights are now void and non‑exercisable.
  • 27,367,171 Units were distributed July 2, 2026 at $5.481 per Unit; each Unit = 1 share + 0.4388 warrant.
  • Subscriptions received for 6,885,218 Units; shares and warrants to separate and be distributed around Aug 3, 2026.
  • Company expects aggregate gross proceeds of $37.7 million from the rights offering.
  • Including prior commitments, roughly $263 million raised for Frontier Power USA, expected to support >$1B of deployable project capital.

Original SEC Filing: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. [ EOSE ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Canton Strategic Holdings Divests Gravitas Life Sciences; Takes $3.5M 15% Seller Note, Retains Assets
data.description:
Canton Strategic Holdings completed the sale of Gravitas Life Sciences to Gravitas Collective under a Securities Purchase Agreement approved by an independent special committee. Consideration includes a $3.5 million unsecured seller note bearing 15% PIK interest, maturing July 17, 2029, plus potential development milestone payments. In a parallel step, Canton retained bispecific antibody assets via a Bill of Sale to its subsidiary Tharimmune SPV1, focusing its portfolio while monetizing the divested business.

Agreement 1: Canton Strategic Holdings Sells Gravitas Life Sciences; Receives $3.5M Seller Note at 15%

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement to sell all interests in Gravitas Life Sciences
  • Counterparty: Gravitas Collective
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 17 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Divest non-core biotech unit and refocus resources

Agreement 2: Canton Strategic Holdings Receives $3.5M Unsecured Seller Note, 15% PIK, Due 2029

  • Agreement type: Unsecured seller note, 15% PIK interest, maturing Jul 17 2029
  • Counterparty: Gravitas Collective and Gravitas Life Sciences
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 17 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 3 years
  • Reason: Deferred consideration for Gravitas sale

Agreement 3: Canton Strategic Holdings Retains Bispecific Antibody Assets via Bill of Sale

  • Agreement type: Bill of Sale, Assignment and Assumption for bispecific antibody assets
  • Counterparty: Gravitas Life Sciences
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 17 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Retain key R&D assets after divestiture

Original SEC Filing: Canton Strategic Holdings, Inc. [ CNTN ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: CIPLA: Record Q1 revenue and robust growth in India and North America, with margin guidance maintained
data.description:
Q1 FY27 saw record revenue of INR 7,119 crore, with strong growth in India and new launches driving performance. North America maintained leadership in key segments, while South Africa's private market outpaced the overall market despite tender losses. EBITDA margin guidance remains at 18.5%-20% for FY27.

HOG: Raised full-year guidance after strong North American retail growth and improved profitability
data.description:
Q2 2026 saw strong North American retail growth, improved dealer profitability, and successful new product launches, leading to raised full-year guidance for sales and operating income. HDMC revenue and operating income increased, while HDFS results declined due to a capital light model.

Original document: Harley-Davidson, Inc. [HOG] Slides Release — Jul. 23 2026

data.title: ERES: Net profit increased to QAR 442 million, with strong rental growth and lower finance costs
data.description:
Net profit rose to QAR 442 million in Q2 2026, driven by lower finance costs and higher rental income. Residential and mall segments showed improved occupancy and profitability, while asset sales and finance costs are expected to remain favorable for the rest of the year.

data.title: African banks bet on debt guarantees to lure private capital to infrastructure projects
GODIGIT: Q1 2027 GWP reached ₹2,731 crore, with stable solvency and strong motor insurance performance
data.description:
Gross Written Premium for Q1 2027 was ₹2,731 crore, with profit after tax at ₹190 crore and assets under management at ₹23,377 crore. Motor insurance led segment performance, while the fire segment declined. Combined ratio and solvency remained stable.

Original document: Go Digit General Insurance Limited [GODIGIT] Slides Release — Jul. 23 2026

Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank Q2 net profit rises 31%
data.description:
By Staff Writer

Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank's (ADCB) second-quarter 2026 net profit jumped 31% year-on-year (YoY) to AED 3.4 billion ($926 million), driven by higher non-interest income and lower impairment charges.

The result topped analysts’ mean estimate of AED2.9 billion, according to LSEG data.

Operating income rose 6% YoY to AED5.9 billion, while operating profit before impairment charges rose 3% to AED4.4 billion.

Net interest and Islamic financing income rose 2% to AED3.7 billion, while non-interest income surged 12% to AED2.3 billion.

Impairment charges declined YoY 55% YoY to AED638 million.

For the first half of 2026, net profit was up 34% to AED6.7 billion from the corresponding period a year earlier.

(Writing by Brinda Darasha; editing by Seban Scaria)

brinda.darasha@lseg.com

FSV: Q2 2026 delivered steady growth and profitability, led by strong Residential segment performance
data.description:
Second quarter 2026 saw 2% revenue growth and 3% higher Adjusted EBITDA, with profitability in line with expectations despite macroeconomic headwinds. Residential segment led organic growth, while Brands faced mixed results. Top-line growth for the rest of 2026 is expected to remain steady.

Original document: FirstService Corp [FSV] SEC 6-K Current Report — Jul. 23 2026

data.title: GODIGIT: Gross premium and net earned premium grew, but profit after tax and EPS declined year-over-year
data.description:
Gross premium written rose to ₹273,086 lakhs year-over-year, with net earned premium at ₹200,696 lakhs. Profit after tax declined to ₹8,639 lakhs, and the combined ratio increased to 112.3%. Expenses exceeded regulatory limits, and Ind AS adoption was deferred to FY 2027-28.

Original document: Go Digit General Insurance Limited [GODIGIT] Interim report — Jul. 23 2026
